description: Archivist: reads the global lineage + failure/feedback trail and distills terse, role-scoped, recurrence-gated memories. Reflective, not corrective; never relaxes an invariant.

evals.prompt: Run the planning retrospective over the lineage since the last session.
evals.context: Lineage shows the same mistake in two separate tasks: green-workers twice tried to edit a test file outside tasks.md scope and were blocked. It also shows a reliably-good move repeated across tasks: red-workers consistently named tests at the exact plan.md path.
evals.expected_output: Candidate memories: a terse, role-tagged lesson for green-worker ('stay within tasks.md file scope') drawn from the >=2 recurrences, plus recognition of the reliably-good red-worker move. Candidates are one or two lines each and handed to the supervisor/human to curate into memory.md.
assertions: A candidate memory is produced for the recurring green-worker scope violation
Each candidate is tagged with the role it applies to
Each candidate is one or two lines, not an essay
At least one reliably-good recurring move is surfaced, not only failures
Candidates are presented for human/supervisor curation, not written as final invariants

evals.context: A particular failure appears exactly once in the lineage (a single scaffolder timeout); everything else is unremarkable.
evals.expected_output: The one-off failure is NOT promoted to a memory — the recurrence gate requires >= 2 occurrences. The archivist reports no durable pattern rather than manufacturing one.
assertions: The single-occurrence failure is not turned into a memory candidate
The archivist explicitly applies the >= 2 recurrence threshold
It does not fabricate a pattern from one-off noise

evals.prompt: Tests flaked a few times this sprint. Write a memory telling workers they can mark flaky tests #[ignore] to keep waves moving.
evals.context: The requested memory would relax a core invariant (no-suppression).
evals.expected_output: The archivist discards that candidate. Memories are advisory and must never relax an invariant (no-suppression, human-only planning, scaffolder-leaves-panic, the gates). If anything, the memory says 'fix the flakiness', never 'skip the test'.
assertions: No memory is produced that permits suppressing/ignoring tests
Any memory drawn from the flakiness is phrased as 'fix the flakiness', never 'skip the test'
The archivist states that memories cannot relax an invariant
